item.title: Kenya Treasury Warns Budget Strain May Force Spending Cuts
item.description: Kenya may be forced to cut spending in its next fiscal budget as rising debt-service costs, limited tax options and tighter borrowing constraints strain the Treasury’s financing plans.

item.title: Zepto Said to Plan June Public Filing for $1 Billion India IPO
item.description: Rapid-commerce firm Zepto Ltd. is preparing to publicly file in the first half of June for an initial public offering that may raise up to $1 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Pope Says AI Should Be Disarmed to Avoid Dominating Humanity
item.description: Pope Leo XIV said artificial intelligence should be “disarmed” to protect humanity from its dangers, adding his voice to a heated debate over the extent to which governments should regulate a technology that is reshaping the world.

item.title: Turkey Rate Hike Bets Rise as Political Noise Adds to Iran Woes
item.description: Investors are increasing bets that Turkey’s central bank will need to raise interest rates, as higher energy costs keep inflation elevated and a political crisis puts pressure on the lira.

item.title: Japan Won’t Add Bonds on Calendar Basis to Fund Extra Budget
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said the government will finance its extra budget without increasing bond issuance on a calendar basis, in an attempt to ease market concerns over government finances.

item.title: Swiss Trader Had Lucrative Role Getting Iraqi Oil Through Hormuz
item.description: A little-known Swiss trading company played a key role in the transit through the Strait of Hormuz of an oil supertanker whose stop-start journey captivated the oil market earlier this month, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Ericsson Quits Suburb Once Touted as Sweden’s Silicon Valley
item.description: Ericsson AB is moving its global headquarters to central Stockholm after more than two decades in Kista, a struggling suburb in the Swedish capital once touted as Sweden’s Silicon Valley.

item.title: BlackRock’s Saigal Sees ‘Sufficient Factors’ to Justify Fed Cut
item.description: The Federal Reserve may have enough reason to justify an interest rate cut rather than a hike under new chairman Kevin Warsh, according to BlackRock Inc.

item.title: What It Takes to Run One of London’s Most Popular Pubs
item.description: A conversation with two co-founders of the Devonshire.

item.title: Odd Lots: How to Run One of London’s Most Popular Pubs (Podcast)
item.description: As our listeners know, restaurants are great microcosms for macro-economic trends. They sit at the intersection of everything from consumer confidence to commodity costs to the labor market. So on our recent visit to London, we wanted to learn about the business of pubs. According to the British Beer and Pub Association, approximately two pubs a day have closed in England during the first quarter of 2026. Could pubs tell us something about larger trends in the British economy? And when it comes

item.title: Ireland Must Address Economic Vulnerabilities, IMF Warns
item.description: Ireland cannot take its fiscal resilience for granted, the International Monetary Fund warned, as unpredictable geopolitical events elevate uncertainty even in one of Europe’s strongest economies.

item.title: China’s Solar Installations Fall for Fourth Straight Month
item.description: China’s new solar installations fell for a fourth straight month in April, underscoring persistent weakness in domestic demand.

item.title: China Trading Tycoon Loses $1.7 Billion in One Day After Curbs
item.description: A Chinese online brokerage tycoon lost more than a quarter of his fortune in a single day after Beijing cracked down on cross-border stock trading to tighten control over capital outflows.

item.title: China Traders Rush for Exit After Cross-Border Flow Crackdown
item.description: Chinese investors are rushing to find alternative ways to buy and sell overseas equities after Beijing launched its most forceful crackdown on illicit cross-border stock trading to stem capital outflows.

item.title: SoftBank Shares Hit Record High With Lift From OpenAI IPO Hopes
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. shares climbed to a record high, spurred by hopes of big returns from the Japanese investor’s stakes in OpenAI and SB Energy Corp., should the two go public.

item.title: Malaysia Reports Fallout From Iran War as Industries Get Hit
item.description: Malaysia is starting to see the economic impact from the Middle East conflict across several sectors, with disruptions expected to deepen in the coming months.

item.title: South Africa’s Pick n Pay Delays Break-Even Target, Flags Risks
item.description: South Africa’s Pick n Pay Stores Ltd. delayed a key turnaround target by a year, tempering optimism that had driven the grocer’s stock higher ahead of the results.

item.title: SoftBank Taps Retail Market Again With $1.6 Billion Yen Bond
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. plans to raise ¥260 billion ($1.6 billion) through another sale of subordinated bonds aimed mainly at individual investors, about two months after a similar retail offering.

item.title: Australia to Include Existing LNG Contracts in Reservation
item.description: Australia’s requirement for liquefied natural gas producers to reserve a fifth of exports for local use is set to apply to all projects and existing contracts, intensifying pressure on them to secure more supply.

item.title: Italy’s Cassa Depositi Plans to Raise Nexi Stake Up to 29.9%
item.description: Italy’s state lender Cassa Depositi e Prestiti SpA plans to raise its stake in Nexi SpA to as much as 29.9%, backing the Italian digital payment company’s long-term strategy.

item.title: NAB Tightens Mortgage Borrowing After Property Tax Break Removed
item.description: National Australia Bank Ltd. has tightened its home lending rules after the Australian government removed a perk that allowed property investors to claim mortgage interest payments as a tax deduction.

item.title: Adnoc’s Tankers Are Slipping Oil, Gas and Fuel Through Hormuz
item.description: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. has been quietly ferrying oil and gas shipments out of the Persian Gulf using its own fleet, apparently circumventing both the Iranian navy and US warships to reach energy-starved customers.

item.title: NTT Docomo Sells Tokyo Land Plots for $371 Million, Joining Boom
item.description: NTT Docomo Inc. sold two plots of land in central Tokyo for about ¥59 billion ($371 million), according to people familiar with the matter, the latest large deal in Japan’s booming real estate market.

item.title: Buyer Says US Allows Ships Sanctioned Over Iran to Be Scrapped
item.description: GMS, a major buyer of ships and offshore vessels for recycling, says it has received the first US permit to purchase carriers sanctioned over Iran.

item.title: Thailand’s Trade Deficit Hits Record $10 Billion as Imports Soar
item.description: Higher imports of capital goods and raw materials, along with soaring oil and gas prices, pushed Thailand’s trade deficit to its widest level on record dating back to 1991.

item.title: Emerging-Market Assets Rise as Oil Drops on US-Iran Deal Hopes
item.description: Emerging-market stocks and currencies rose as oil prices declined on signs that the US and Iran were nearing a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Japan Bonds Face Homegrown Downside Risks Even as Oil Retreats
item.description: A gauge of risk compensation for holding government bonds has risen fastest in Japan among major markets since the US-Iran war began, pointing to local factors that may continue to weigh on Japanese bonds even if energy prices retreat.

item.title: AI Rally Drives Best Run in Global Momentum Stocks on Record
item.description: The AI boom is giving momentum investors their best return in decades, as the world’s hottest stocks power ahead despite worries over potentially slower growth due to the Iran war.

item.title: Sri Lanka Rupee to Recover From Plunge as Oil to Ease, BMI Says
item.description: The Sri Lankan rupee is set to recover from its slide by the end of this year as oil prices are set to decline and as the central bank raises interest rates, according to BMI.

item.title: Bank SMBC Indonesia Sells $1.1 Billion in Pension Loans to BTN
item.description: PT Bank SMBC Indonesia, a unit of Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp, is selling 19.9 trillion rupiah ($1.1 billion) worth of pension loans as it tilts away from its legacy business.
